name: Deploy Railway (real handler — serviceInstanceRedeploy via GraphQL)

parameters.body: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: 'mutation Redeploy($serviceId: String!, $environmentId: String!) { serviceInstanceRedeploy(serviceId: $serviceId, environmentId: $environmentId) }', variables: { serviceId: $('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.service_id, environmentId: ($('Compute Skills Router HMAC').first().json._meta.environment_id || $env['RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT_ID'] || '') } }) }}

_comment: Real handler. Pattern mirrors github-pr.json (PR #12) and create-adr.json (PR #11): webhook → HMAC validate (R-02 fail-closed) → Respond 200 in parallel → ADR-0003 sign → Call Skills Router → Routing Gate IF (skill==='deploy-railway' && status!=='pending_approval') → Railway GraphQL POST (serviceInstanceRedeploy(serviceId, environmentId)) → Telegram reply. Required env vars: DEPLOY_RAILWAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET, SKILLS_ROUTER_URL, SKILLS_ROUTER_SECRET, RAILWAY_API_TOKEN (Railway account-scope token, same one used by bootstrap.yml's variableCollectionUpsert), RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT_ID (default fallback when payload omits environment_id), TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN. Inbound payload contract: { service_id: string (required, Railway service ID), environment_id?: string (defaults to RAILWAY_ENVIRONMENT_ID env var), chat_id?: number|string, user_id?: number|string }. The mutation is non-destructive: serviceInstanceRedeploy re-runs the latest deployment without altering source or config. SKILL.md classifies this as requires_approval=false.
